business.headline: Carlsberg eyes India stock market listing; confidential IPO filing submittedCarlsberg said it has confidentially filed for an IPO of its Indian business. The proposed listing would allow a partial stake sale, subject to approvals and market conditions.

ITR 2026: Do you need to file two income tax returns? Tax department explainsThe new Income-tax Act has changed the way taxpayers talk about return filing, replacing "Assessment Year" with "Tax Year". But does that mean you'll have to file two ITRs? Here's what the tax department says.

Sensex, Nifty end higher on IT rally; HCLTech surges nearly 6%The BSE Sensex rose 261.79 points, or 0.34%, to settle at 77,763.91, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 95.15 points, or 0.39%, to close at 24,270.85.

IRDAI is finally going after the real reason behind insurance mis-sellingIRDAI is discussing a shift from upfront commissions to staggered payouts with the insurance industry. The proposed changes target mis-selling incentives and could tighten disclosure across distributors.

New EPFO Scheme: Will your employer contribute only Rs 1,800 to PF?The new EPFO Scheme, 2026 has raised an important question among salaried employees: will employers now limit their PF contribution to just Rs 1,800 a month? Here's what the new rules actually say.

Over Rs 9,300 crore lies unclaimed in 31 lakh EPF accounts, enough to build 3 IITsAn RTI response revealed that Rs 9,330 crore remains unclaimed in 30.91 lakh inoperative EPF accounts across India. The disclosure comes as the new EPF Scheme, 2026 takes effect, while details on Aadhaar-linked and high-value accounts remain undisclosed.

Planning to buy a plot in Noida? Read this Noida Authority warning firstThe Noida Authority has warned people against buying or selling land under specified khasra numbers in three villages. It said the parcels are acquired or notified land where illegal construction could bring financial and legal trouble.

Missed your EMI by a day? Here's how it can affect your credit scoreDid you miss your EMI by a day and instantly worry about your CIBIL score? Before you panic, here's what actually happens behind the scenes.

AI mega deal: Why HCLTech shares jump nearly 6% today?At around 11 am, HCLTech was trading nearly 6% higher. The rally also helped lift the broader Nifty IT index for the second straight session, even as AI-heavy markets in South Korea and Taiwan witnessed a sharp selloff.

Air India cuts fuel surcharge on North America, Europe flights as oil easesAir India has reduced temporary fuel surcharges on select long-haul routes from July 1. The move follows easing oil prices after the West Asia conflict had pushed up operating costs.
Sensex opens 400 points higher, Nifty above 24,300; HCLTech up 4%The BSE Sensex rose 507 points to 78,009.37, while the NSE Nifty50 gained nearly 170 points to trade at 24,346.25 in early trading.

Is the EPFO portal working today? Here's what PF subscribers should knowEPFO has said member and employer services are expected to resume from July 3 after another maintenance extension. The prolonged upgrade has delayed PF claims, passbook access and compliance tasks for nearly a week.

Zero tax in Form 16? You may still have to file an ITR. Here's whyEven if your employer has deducted no income tax because you qualify for the rebate under Section 87A, you may still be legally required to file an ITR in several situations.

EPF Scheme 2026: What employees and employers need to knowWith around eight crore active EPFO subscribers, the government hopes the updated scheme will make provident fund management simpler, more efficient and better suited to a digital-first workplace.

Will petrol, diesel prices be cut? Hardeep Puri says here's when it's possiblePuri explained that although international crude oil prices have fallen in recent weeks, oil companies are still refining crude that was purchased when prices were much higher during the West Asia conflict.

Centre eyes LIC, PSU stake sales to cushion strain from oil crisis: ReportThe Centre is speeding up stake sales in LIC, Hindustan Zinc and other state-run firms. The move is aimed at creating fiscal room as oil price volatility pressures government finances.

Sensex jumps 579 points as IT stocks shine; Nifty closes above 24,175The BSE Sensex climbed 579.48 points, or 0.75%, to close at 77,502.12, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 169.85 points, or 0.71%, to settle at 24,175.70.

AI rally cracks: Why Asian markets are crashing while Indian IT stocks rallyAsian chip stocks slumped after reports stoked fears of excess AI computing capacity. Indian IT shares, more tied to software spending, rose as investors weighed a different AI demand story.

Want to save more tax under new regime? Ask your employer about this NPS benefitWith ITR filing in full swing, our 'KNOW YOUR TAX' series helps you understand the tax benefits that still matter. This edition explains how an employer's NPS contribution can help salaried employees save tax while building a retirement corpus.
Mehli Mistry steps down from Ratan Tata's family officeMistry's resignation from RNT Associates comes months after he was removed from Tata Trusts in November 2025.

entertainment.headline: After years of censorship delays, Diljit Dosanjh's Punjab '95 streams as Satluj Actor Diljit Dosanjh's long-delayed film, Punjab '95 has been released on OTT, as Satluj. The renamed streaming debut follows years of censorship disputes and festival disruption.

Super Subbu review: Bold subject, good laughs, but an underwhelming finishRelease Date:Thu Jul 02, 2026 Super Subbu series review: Director Mallik Ram's Super Subbu, Netflix's first Telugu original series, stars Sundeep Kishan as a sex education officer posted to a remote Telangana village. The seven-episode drama is funny, warm and says something real - until the second half starts asking questions it has no intention of answering.

Salman Khan's Maatrubhumi faces certification delay: What we know so far Actor Salman Khan's Maatrubhumi has reportedly run into a fresh hurdle after the CBFC put its clearance certificate on hold. The makers are yet to confirm the development or announce a new release date.

Gaurav Khanna to enter Lock Upp 2 after wife Akanksha's divorce announcement? Reports suggest actor Gaurav Khanna may enter Lock Upp after his actor wife Akanksha Chamola spoke about their divorce. The unconfirmed buzz has deepened debate over the revelation and heightened curiosity around an on-camera response.

Latent 2: Samay Raina to share stage with his 'comedy heroes' in ep 2. See lineup Samay Raina has announced Kiku Sharda, Chandan Prabhakar and Harsh Limbachiyaa for the second episode of India's Got Latent Season 2. The line-up carries added weight for Raina, who says the trio shaped his comedy and remain his heroes.

Rao Bahadur review: Satyadev carries a film that takes too long to find itselfRelease Date:Fri Jul 03, 2026 Rao Bahadur movie review: Director Maha's Rao Bahadur, starring Satyadev, Vikas Muppala and Deepa Thomas, is a psychological drama that talks about lineage, prejudice and skin colour obsession. The film, built on a theatrical setup, takes a while to warm up but leaves you with plenty of afterthoughts.

Yash once vowed to avoid intimate scenes. Then he did Toxic Yash's film Toxic has drawn criticism after its Ladies and Ladies teaser was labelled vulgar. The backlash has revived his earlier remarks about only doing scenes he could watch with his parents.

72nd National Film Awards likely to be announced next week: Sources Government officials said the 72nd National Film Awards are likely to be announced next week, not today. The clarification has eased speculation as anticipation builds around films certified in 2024.

Taylor-Travis Wedding LIVE: Celebrities start reaching Madison Square Garden Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce Wedding Live Updates: Singer Taylor Swift and her fiance, NFL star Travis Kelce's wedding has become the most talked-about event of the weekend. The Death of Robin Hood review: Hugh Jackman anchors bleak thriller on iconic outlawRelease Date:Fri Jul 03, 2026 The Death of Robin Hood review: Actor Hugh Jackman's wounded Robin Hood seeks refuge at a remote priory, where Sister Brigid and Margaret draw him into moments of care and reckoning. The film uses that encounter to probe guilt, legacy and violence. Does the film live up to expectations? Here is our review.

Mithila Palkar: Deep-rooted conditioning is why we still struggle with consent Actor Mithila Palkar, in an exclusive interaction, spoke about choosing Super Subbu and its handling of sex education and consent. She said deep-rooted conditioning continues to shape how society understands boundaries and "no means no".

150 guests, couples' fav food items: Exclusive details from Aamir Khan's wedding Actor Aamir Khan and his partner Gauri Spratt will marry at home on July 5 in a private ceremony. The couple have kept the guest list small, chosen the menu themselves and sought blessings for their new chapter.

Graamaayana review: Vinay Rajkumar's rural drama is sincere, but lacks impactRelease Date:Fri Jul 03, 2026 Graamaayana movie review: Directed by Devanuru Chandru, the rural drama starring Vinay Rajkumar, Megha Shetty, and Gopalkrishna Deshpande impresses in parts but falls short of leaving a lasting impact.

Sunil Pal claims he turned down Rs 25 lakh for Samay Raina's India's Got Latent 2 Sunil Pal says Samay Raina invited him to India's Got Latent 2 with a Rs 25 lakh offer. The claim deepens their long-running public feud over comedy and abusive language.

Summer '36 review: This French period mystery starts with a bang. Then nothingRelease Date:Wed Jul 01, 2026 Summer '36 review: Set on the French Riviera in 1936, it opens with a gripping murder and plenty of intrigue. But despite its gorgeous period setting, solid performances and atmospheric storytelling, the mystery gets weighed down by too many characters and tangled subplots.

Vijay's Jana Nayagan yet to receive censor board clearance: Source Sources close to Vijay's Jana Nayagan said the film is still awaiting CBFC clearance despite reports of an A certificate. The release date will be announced only after certification, even as speculation over a late July release continues.

Nagabandham review: A mythic fantasy adventure lost in its own mazeRelease Date:Fri Jul 03, 2026 Nagabandham review: The film sets up a mythological hunt for two sacred relics and a hidden gateway. Its scale and visual ambition are overshadowed by weak writing and thin emotional stakes.
72nd National Film Awards: When and where to watch The 72nd National Film Awards is liekly to be announced next week, with the results to be streamed live online.

Alpha review: Alia is alpha, the rest is betaRelease Date:Fri Jul 03, 2026 Alpha movie review: The story follows Sita, a kidnapped child turned assassin, as her revenge mission spirals through twists and family fallout. Alia Bhatt's action holds the film together, but the melodrama and clutter blunt its impact.

general news.headline: Govt monitoring drought risk, says Amit Shah amid El Nino-linked rainfall deficitWith June recording one of India's driest spells on record and the IMD forecasting below-normal rainfall in July, Union Home Minister Amit Shah reviewed drought preparedness, directing ministries to coordinate with states to protect agriculture, water resources and essential supplies.

Ritabrata faction 'takes over' TMC HQ in Kolkata as battle with Mamata escalatesThe crisis within the 28-year-old Trinamool Congress deepened after a majority of its MLAs backed Ritabrata Banerjee's challenge to Mamata Banerjee's leadership and elected senior legislator Arup Roy as the party's chairperson during a special session convened by the dissident camp last month.

AUS 1-1 EGY: Egypt seal maiden Round of 16 spot after penaltiesAustralia vs Egypt live score and updates, FIFA World Cup 2026: History has been made as Egypt defeated Australia 4-2 on penalties after a gripping 1-1 draw, sealing their first-ever place in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16. Mohamed Salah's side held their nerve from the spot to script a landmark night for Egyptian football.

Will sit outside Gadkari's house, says Tehseen Poonawalla ahead of E20 protestIndia is set to witness its first street protest over the sale of ethanol-blended petrol. A protest led by entrepreneur Tehseen Poonawalla is planned at Delhi's Jantar Mantar on Sunday. What if police permission isn't granted? Poonawalla shares his alternative plan, in a light-hearted manner, but with steely resolve.

Did we get Haldighati winner wrong for 450 years?It is commonly accepted that Akbar won the Battle of Haldighati. But did Maharana Pratap win the war? A fresh reading of the 450-year-old battle amid US President Donald Trump's repeated claims that the US won the war against Iran.

Kamikaze drones, guided missiles among Rs 52,000-crore defence buys clearedDefence procurement proposals worth Rs 52,000 crore, including anti-drone systems, missiles, jet-powered kamikaze drones and advanced air defence platforms received clearance to move forward.

Aryna Sabalenka sets up Naomi Osaka blockbuster, Jannik Sinner storms into Round 4Aryna Sabalenka defeated Jelena Ostapenko in straight sets to set up a blockbuster Round of 16 clash against Naomi Osaka, while defending champion Jannik Sinner brushed aside Jenson Brooksby to continue his Wimbledon title defence.

I usually read stories about myself: Trump on Usha Vance's podcast for childrenAppearing on Second Lady Usha Vance's podcast to read a picture book for young audiences, US President Donald Trump veered off script with jokes about ex-presidents, his own looks, his weight, and even the new White House ballroom.

Khamenei's funeral a show of strength, world leaders attend as Mojtaba stays awayFor now, attention remains fixed on Tehran, as the state projects unity around a leader it portrays as having guided Iran through war, isolation, and crisis. Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's death has become the centrepiece of a highly symbolic political farewell.

Ketan murder case: Siya and Chetan used code words in chats, say Pune copsPune fort murder case: Police say accused Siya Goyal and her boyfriend Chetan Chaudhary used code words in their chats in the murder of businessman Ketan Agarwal. Investigators have seized Siya's second phone as they piece together the events leading to Ketan's murder.

Govt asks Google, Apple to remove apps that can shut down e-rickshaw batteriesThe Centre has directed Google and Apple to remove seven apps allegedly capable of remotely disabling batteries in e-rickshaws and other electric vehicles, citing concerns over misuse, vehicle safety and unauthorised interference with EV battery systems.

Smuggling of human placenta for anti-ageing serum? 3 Chinese men arrested in PakAuthorities in Pakistan have busted an alleged transnational human placenta smuggling network in Islamabad, leading to the arrest of five suspects, including three Chinese nationals. According to reports, the placentas were illegally processed before being smuggled to Vietnam, where authorities believe they were used in making anti-ageing products and injections.

Madam is sleeping: Ex-envoy recalls 1991 hijack story to slam Pakistan leadersFormer Singapore envoy Bilahari Kausikan said Pakistani politicians were nothing but a 'waste of time'. To substantiate his remark, Kausikan narrated an incident in 1991 when a Singapore Airlines flight was hijacked by Pakistani terrorists.

Pak Deputy PM's relative among 4 arrested for raping 2 European women in LahorePolice have arrested four men, including a relative of Pakistan's Deputy PM and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar, on charges of kidnapping and gang-raping two European women in Lahore last month.

No more heatwave headlines: Monsoon rain to drench India on SaturdayThe IMD has forecast heavy to extremely heavy rainfall, thunderstorms and gusty winds across several states as the southwest monsoon remains active through the first week of July.

AI to fake clean-ups, GPS spoofing to skip work: 4 Gurugram civic staffers sackedFour contractual civic employees in Gurugram were terminated after using AI-generated images and other techniques to falsely show that garbage had been cleared, despite never visiting the locations.

Google was Aravind Srinivas's middle-class dream. Perplexity made him CEOBefore leading an AI startup, Aravind Srinivas thought success meant one thing. A stable job at Google. Nothing more, nothing less. Today, his definition of ambition looks very different.

Indus treaty remains in abeyance over cross-border terror: Govt amid Pak rhetoricReiterating its position on the Indus Waters Treaty, India said the pact will remain in abeyance until Pakistan ends cross-border terrorism. The MEA also condemned Pakistan's strikes in Afghanistan and sought a probe into an Indian seafarer's death in Venezuela.

Body of man missing for 45 days found buried under bathroom tiles, wife arrestedA 44-year-old Agra man who was reported missing for 45 days was found buried beneath newly laid bathroom tiles inside his own home, after police uncovered the body during a search triggered by suspicious inconsistencies in the investigation.

Founder's moving post for son who traded corporate comfort for deep-sea diving"He didn't need me to protect him from this choice. He needed me to trust," education platform Careers360's Maheshwer Peri said.

Who's OP Shah, the sutradhaar of the India-Pakistan peace letter?OP Shah coordinated a joint India-Pakistan appeal urging talks, de-escalation and renewed engagement on Jammu and Kashmir. His initiative saw over 100 prominent people signing a letter to Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi and his Pakistani counterpart, Shehbaz Sharif. Here's more about OP Shah, the man behind the letter that has divided opinions in India.

69-year-old defrauded 300+ luxury hotels across India in 36 years, arrestedRaipur Police said John checked into the Hyatt Hotel on June 25 and left on the morning of June 27 without completing the checkout process. He allegedly failed to pay an outstanding bill of Rs 63,755 and also took away a laptop worth around Rs 1.48 lakh that had been provided to him on request.

sports.headline: ARG vs CPV Live: Messi in spotlight as Cape Verde look to extend fairytale run Argentina vs Cabo Verde live, FIFA World Cup: Defending champions Argentina enter the FIFA World Cup knockout stage as favorites, but Cape Verde have already defied expectations to reach the Round of 32. England vs Mexico kick-off time may change as FIFA monitors storm threat England's World Cup Round of 16 clash against Mexico could kick off earlier than scheduled after FIFA opened discussions over severe weather forecasts, adding another challenge to an already daunting trip to the high-altitude Estadio Azteca.

Novak Djokovic is like MS Dhoni, knows how to handle pressure: India all-rounder India all-rounder Deepti Sharma has compared Novak Djokovic and MS Dhoni, saying both share an exceptional calmness under pressure that defines their greatness.

Djokovic throws Federer cheeky challenge after equalling Wimbledon record Novak Djokovic matched Roger Federer's record of men's singles wins at Wimbledon before cheekily challenging the Swiss legend to a winner-takes-all showdown after beating Arthur Rinderknech in four sets.

Why India are delaying Vaibhav Sooryavanshi debut? India bowling coach explains India bowling coach Morne Morkel has defended the decision to delay Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's debut, saying it would be unfair to drop in-form openers Abhishek Sharma and Sanju Samson.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi flying high, but England have plans to stop him: Sam Curran Sam Curran praised Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's fearless rise but said England are ready with plans to stop him, adding that handling the pressure of representing India will be the teenager's toughest challenge.

England name 16-man squad for India ODI series, pace duo earn recalls England have named a 16-man squad for the three-match ODI series against India, recalling pacers Gus Atkinson and Saqib Mahmood while handing Sussex all-rounder James Coles his maiden ODI call-up.

Sooryavanshi as talented as Tendulkar, Kohli, but give him time: Kapil Dev Kapil Dev compared teenage batting sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's talent to that of Sachin Tendulkar and Virat Kohli but urged fans to give the 15-year-old time to develop instead of burdening him with expectations.

Day 5 LIVE: Djokovic, Sinner into Round of 16, Sabalenka beats OstapenkoWimbledon 2026 Day 5 Live Updates: Novak Djokovic has stormed into the Round of 16 after beating Arthur Rinderknech on Centre Court. Sleep needed: How England are planning to save themselves from rowdy Mexico fansEngland are set to stay under enhanced security ahead of their FIFA World Cup Round of 16 clash against Mexico. The move comes in the fear of the fact that rowdy home fans might not let them sleep the night ahead of the match.

India set to criminalise doping, government plans sweeping legal overhaulThe government is planning to amend the National Anti-Doping Act to introduce criminal action against coaches, support staff and others involved in facilitating doping, sources told India Today.

Cristiano Ronaldo addresses retirement rumours spread by sisterCristiano Ronaldo has addressed the retirement rumours spread by his own sister Katia. Speaking after Portugal's 2-1 comeback win against Croatia, Ronaldo has clarified his sister's comments.

One goal for Cristiano Ronaldo, one headache for PortugalCristiano Ronaldo finally scored his first-ever FIFA World Cup knockout-stage goal as Portugal fought back to beat Croatia 2-1. But Goncalo Ramos' late winner and Roberto Martinez's bold decision to substitute his captain have opened up a fresh tactical debate ahead of a blockbuster Round of 16 clash against Spain.

India series win boosts Ireland's status in international cricket: O'DonnellIreland's landmark T20I series win over reigning world champions India has transformed how the team is viewed internationally, with Cricket Ireland president Bryan O'Donnell saying the success will help secure bigger opportunities and stronger opposition.

FIFA World Cup: Cristiano Ronaldo, Portugal take over the streets of TorontoPortugal's dramatic FIFA World Cup Round of 32 victory over Croatia sparked jubilant scenes in Toronto on Thursday night, with hundreds of supporters gathering outside the team's hotel. The Portugal team also joined in, leading the chants from their team hotel.

Mahrez's final World Cup campaign cut short as clinical Switzerland reach R16Switzerland made it into their fourth straight Round of 16 in the FIFA World Cup. The clinical Swiss beat Algeria 2-0 in Vancouver, cutting short Riyad Mahrez's final World Cup campaign.

Nagelsmann quits Germany job after World Cup debacle, Klopp favourite to take overJulian Nagelsmann has stepped down as Germany's head coach after the four-time world champions crashed out of the FIFA World Cup 2026 in the Round of 32, ending his nearly three-year tenure in charge.

Goodbye 21 points: India racing ahead with badminton's new scoring systemThe Badminton Association of India has proactively fast-tracked the BWF's revolutionary 3x15 scoring system into its domestic circuit from July 2026, ensuring Indian shuttlers master the high-intensity format well before its 2027 global rollout.

Wimbledon Day 5 order of play: Djokovic, Osaka headline third-round actionThe third round of Wimbledon begins on Friday, July 3, with Novak Djokovic, Naomi Osaka and Aryna Sabalenka leading the day's action, while defending champion Jannik Sinner is set for a surprise switch from Centre Court to No. 1 Court for his third-round clash.

Were Croatia robbed? Why VAR disallowed dramatic equaliser vs PortugalFIFA World Cup: Croatia's campaign ended in agony after a dramatic stoppage-time equaliser against Portugal was ruled out following a VAR review in their Round of 32 clash on Thursday.

Ronaldo consoles Modric as Portugal knock Croatia out in late VAR dramaFIFA World Cup: Cristiano Ronaldo consoled a tearful Luka Modric after Portugal edged Croatia 2-1 in a dramatic Round of 32 clash on Thursday, ending the Croatian captain's campaign in heartbreaking fashion.

SUI 2-0 ALG at Full Time: Embolo, Ndoye score as the Swiss reach R16Switzerland (SUI) vs Algeria (ALG) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: A clinical Switzerland team go through to the Round of 16 with a 2-0 win over Algeria. Embolo and Ndoye score in either half as the Swiss march on to the next knockout round of the World Cup.

Cristiano Ronaldo's World Cup dream lives on after Portugal survive CroatiaFIFA World Cup 2026: Portugal came from behind to beat Croatia 2-1 in Toronto, with Cristiano Ronaldo scoring from the spot and Goncalo Ramos heading a stoppage-time winner. The result sends Portugal into a Round of 16 clash with Spain and leaves Roberto Martinez weighing a major selection call.

Ronaldo in tears wearing No. 21 as Portugal honour Jota with epic win vs CroatiaCristiano Ronaldo wore Diogo Jota's No. 21 shirt and left the pitch in tears after Portugal's dramatic 2-1 win over Croatia on Thursday, as the national team paid an emotional tribute to their late teammate on the first anniversary of his death.

Pints till penalties! PM Starmer lets pubs stay open for England vs MexicoFIFA World Cup 2026: The UK government has allowed pubs and bars in England and Wales to stay open until 5 a.m. for England's World Cup Round of 16 match against Mexico. The move cuts red tape for venues and gives the hospitality trade a timely lift for a high-stakes late-night fixture.

POR 2-1 CRO Highlights: Portugal are through to the last 16 after late VAR dramaPortugal (POR) vs Croatia (CRO) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: (POR 2-1 CRO) Cristiano Ronaldo scored the first World Cupknockout-stage goal of his career, Goncalo Ramos netted a stoppage-time header and Portugal got a last-second break on an offside call to beat Croatia 2-1 on Thursday and set up a last-16 clash with Spain.

Spain too good for Austria, advance to last 16 with easeFIFA World Cup 2026: Spain cruised into the Round of 16 with a dominant 3-0 victory over Austria, as Mikel Oyarzabal scored twice and Pedro Porro added his first international goal. Luis de la Fuente's side controlled the contest from start to finish and will now face either Portugal or Croatia in the next round.

England Women bury South Africa ghosts, set up blockbuster final vs AustraliaWomen's T20 World Cup, ENGW vs SAW: England beat South Africa by 40 runs and set up a final with Australia on Sunday at the iconic Lord's Cricket Ground in London.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
